audits.required_next: Publish the quorum map pre-data and obtain a named collaboration willing to sign it.
audits.interpretation: mandatory_archive_and_escrow_release preserves the full T171/T173 guardian set under threshold control and is the only live quorum policy in the current fixture family.

audits.required_next: Rewrite the release quorum so every authorized challenge-window row-release coalition includes both archive custody and escrow.
audits.interpretation: optional_escrow_two_of_three_release is null because its authorized coalitions can bypass archive_custodian, escrow_custodian on critical challenge-window actions.

audits.required_next: Rewrite the release quorum so every authorized challenge-window row-release coalition includes both archive custody and escrow.
audits.interpretation: optional_archive_three_of_four_release is null because its authorized coalitions can bypass archive_custodian on critical challenge-window actions.

audits.required_next: Rewrite the revocation and audit quorums so trust audit and escrow cannot be bypassed on critical challenge-window actions.
audits.interpretation: optional_trust_two_of_three_revocation is null because its authorized coalitions can bypass trust_auditor on critical challenge-window actions.

audits.required_next: Rewrite the release quorum so every authorized challenge-window row-release coalition includes both archive custody and escrow.
audits.interpretation: global_three_of_five_multisig is null because its authorized coalitions can bypass archive_custodian, escrow_custodian, trust_auditor on critical challenge-window actions.

strongest_claim: Threshold or multisig root control does not rescue Q1B merely by naming escrow or trust audit as possible signers. For the surviving T171/T173 claim-review route, every authorized challenge-window row-release quorum must include both archive custody and escrow, and every authorized revocation quorum must include both trust audit and escrow.
improved: T175 removes a realistic governance loophole from T161/T173. The repo can now evaluate threshold-key or multisig stories without collapsing them either into naive shared-root failure or into unexamined 'distributed control' optimism.
weakened: This weakens Q1B again. A collaboration does not clear the claim-review route by putting escrow or trust on one branch of a quorum tree. If any authorized release or revocation coalition can bypass those guardians, independent challenge rights are only rhetorical.
falsification_condition: T175 fails if a threshold-controlled row-release or revocation policy should still count for Q1B even though some authorized coalition omits archive custody, escrow, or trust audit from the critical challenge-window actions they are meant to guard.
q1b_update: Q1B remains externally blocked. A threshold-key or multisig workflow is admissible only when escrow is a mandatory signer in every authorized challenge-window release and revocation quorum, archive custody is mandatory in every release quorum, and trust audit is mandatory in every revocation or audit quorum.
claim_ledger_update: Add T175 to Q1B: threshold or multisig control is null whenever challenge-window release or revocation can be authorized by a coalition that bypasses archive custody, escrow, or trust audit. Those guardians must be mandatory members of every authorized critical quorum, not optional signers.
open_blocker: No named detector workflow in the repo currently publishes the critical release and revocation quorum map needed to show that escrow and trust are mandatory rather than optional guardians.
recommended_next: Update the Q1B handoff to require a pre-data quorum map for challenge-window row release, audit attestation, and revocation. If a realistic collaboration can only offer optional-escrow or optional-trust multisig, demote Q1B below the active frontier.
